waikiki.name: Waikiki
highlights: Iconic crescent beach with Diamond Head views
World-class shopping along Kalakaua Avenue
Legendary surf breaks for beginners and pros
Vibrant nightlife and dining scene
Duke Kahanamoku statue and beach culture
waikiki.transport: Fly into Honolulu International Airport (HNL), 20 minutes by taxi or TheBus Route 19/20. Waikiki Trolley covers main tourist areas. Most attractions walkable within the district.
tips: Book hotels on the Diamond Head side for quieter beaches
Free hula shows at Kuhio Beach most evenings
Rent a surfboard from local shops rather than hotel concierge for better rates
Visit the International Market Place for upscale shopping with open-air ambiance
Early morning is best for beach photos with fewer crowds

north-shore.name: North Shore
highlights: Pipeline and Sunset Beach — world-famous big wave surf spots
Haleiwa town with art galleries and shave ice
Sea turtle sightings at Laniakea Beach
Farm tours and tropical fruit stands
Shark cage diving adventures
north-shore.transport: 45-minute drive from Waikiki via H-1 and H-2. TheBus Route 52 runs from Honolulu. Limited parking at popular beaches in winter surf season — arrive early.
tips: Winter months (November–February) bring massive swells — watch from the beach if you're not an expert
Matsumoto Shave Ice is iconic but Giovanni's Shrimp Truck is the must-eat
Sunset Beach is calmer in summer and great for swimming
Visit the Dole Plantation on the way up for pineapple treats
Bring reef-safe sunscreen — it's the law in Hawaii

pearl-harbor.name: Pearl Harbor
highlights: USS Arizona Memorial over the sunken battleship
Battleship Missouri where Japan's surrender was signed
USS Bowfin Submarine Museum
Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum on Ford Island
Deeply moving historical experience
pearl-harbor.transport: 20 minutes west of Waikiki by car. TheBus Routes 20/42 stop nearby. Many tour operators offer round-trip shuttles from Waikiki hotels.
tips: Reserve free USS Arizona Memorial tickets online at recreation.gov well in advance
No bags, purses, or backpacks allowed — use the on-site storage lockers ($5)
Arrive by 7 AM to beat the crowds and heat
Allow at least 4 hours to see everything, a full day for all exhibits
Wear respectful clothing — this is an active military base and memorial

diamond-head.name: Diamond Head
highlights: Iconic volcanic crater hike with panoramic views
360-degree vistas of Waikiki, Honolulu, and the Pacific
Historic WWII military bunkers at the summit
One of Hawaii's most photographed landmarks
Moderate 1.6-mile round trip trail
diamond-head.transport: 10-minute drive from Waikiki. TheBus Route 23 stops at the crater entrance. Reservations required for entry — book online at gostateparks.hawaii.gov.
tips: Start early (6:00–6:30 AM) to avoid heat and crowds
Bring a flashlight for the dark tunnel section near the summit
Wear proper footwear — the trail has uneven steps and steep sections
Entry reservations are required and fill up days in advance
Bring plenty of water — there's no shade on most of the trail

kailua.name: Kailua
highlights: Kailua Beach — consistently rated among America's best beaches
Lanikai Pillbox hike with sunrise views
Kayaking to the Mokulua Islands
Charming downtown with boutiques and cafes
Laid-back local community atmosphere
kailua.transport: 30-minute drive from Waikiki through the Pali Highway tunnel. TheBus Route 56/57. Limited vacation rental options due to local regulations — check current rules.
tips: Lanikai Pillbox hike at sunrise is unforgettable — bring a headlamp
Rent kayaks from Kailua Beach Adventures to paddle to the Mokes
Street parking fills up fast on weekends — arrive before 9 AM
Buzz's Original Steakhouse is a beloved local institution
Respect the residential neighborhood — noise and parking rules are enforced

honolulu.name: Honolulu
highlights: Iolani Palace — the only royal palace in the United States
Bishop Museum for Hawaiian history and culture
Chinatown arts district with galleries and restaurants
Ala Moana Center — world's largest open-air mall
Vibrant food scene from plate lunches to fine dining
honolulu.transport: Honolulu International Airport (HNL) is the main gateway. Skyline rail connects airport to Ala Moana. TheBus network covers the metro area. Ride-shares widely available.
tips: First Friday Honolulu in Chinatown is a vibrant art walk with food and music
The Bishop Museum is essential for understanding Hawaiian culture
Try a plate lunch at Rainbow Drive-In or Helena's Hawaiian Food
Visit the Honolulu Museum of Art for a world-class collection in a beautiful setting
Parking downtown is expensive — use TheBus or the new Skyline rail

lahaina.name: Lahaina
highlights: Historic whaling town with Front Street charm
The famous Banyan Tree — one of the largest in the US
Prime whale watching from December to April
Gateway to Maui's west side beaches
Rich cultural heritage and art galleries
lahaina.transport: Fly into Kahului Airport (OGG), 45-minute drive west on Highway 30. West Maui is being rebuilt after the 2023 wildfire — check current access and business status before visiting.
tips: The community is rebuilding after the August 2023 wildfire — visit respectfully and support local businesses
Whale season (December–April) offers spectacular humpback sightings from shore
Book a snorkel trip to Molokini Crater from Lahaina Harbor
Old Lahaina Luau is widely considered the most authentic luau on Maui
Check restoration progress and open areas before planning your visit

haleakala.name: Haleakala
highlights: Sunrise above the clouds at 10,023 feet
Massive volcanic crater with alien-like landscape
Silversword plants found nowhere else on Earth
World-class stargazing after sunset
Downhill biking from summit to sea level
haleakala.transport: 2-hour drive from Kahului up winding Highway 378. Sunrise viewing requires advance reservations through recreation.gov ($1 per reservation plus $30 park entry per vehicle).
tips: Book sunrise reservations 60 days in advance — they sell out immediately
Temperatures at the summit can be 30°F colder than sea level — bring warm layers
The Sliding Sands Trail into the crater is otherworldly but strenuous
Downhill bike tours are thrilling but research operators carefully for safety records
Sunset is equally stunning and doesn't require a reservation

road-to-hana.name: Road to Hana
highlights: 620 curves and 59 bridges through tropical paradise
Countless waterfalls and swimming holes
Black sand beach at Waianapanapa State Park
Bamboo forest and lush rainforest hikes
Authentic Hawaiian small-town culture in Hana
road-to-hana.transport: Starts from Kahului heading east on Highway 36/360. Allow a full day. Waianapanapa State Park requires parking reservations. The road beyond Hana (backside) may be unpaved and rental car agreements may not cover it.
tips: Start by 7 AM to beat traffic and have time for stops
Download an audio tour app — it times narration to your GPS location
Twin Falls is the first stop and gets very crowded — consider skipping for later falls
Pack snacks and lunch — food options are limited and expensive along the route
Pull over at turnouts to let faster traffic pass — it's common courtesy

wailea.name: Wailea
highlights: Five pristine crescent beaches along the coast
Luxury resorts including Four Seasons and Grand Wailea
Wailea Beach Path — 1.5-mile oceanfront walk
Championship golf courses with ocean views
Whale watching right from the beach in winter
wailea.transport: 30-minute drive south from Kahului Airport. The Shops at Wailea serve as the area hub. Limited public transit — a rental car is recommended.
tips: All beaches in Hawaii are public — you can access Wailea's beaches even without a resort stay
Walk the Wailea Beach Path at sunset for spectacular views
Monkeypod Kitchen and Lineage are excellent dining options at The Shops
Visit nearby Makena (Big Beach) for a more local, less resort feel
Morning snorkeling at Ulua Beach often reveals sea turtles

kona.name: Kona
highlights: World-famous Kona coffee farm tours
Manta ray night snorkeling and diving
Historic Kailua Village with oceanfront dining
Deep-sea fishing capital of Hawaii
Ironman World Championship host city
kona.transport: Fly into Kona International Airport (KOA) on the Big Island's west coast. Alii Drive is the main strip. A rental car is essential for exploring the Kona Coast.
tips: Book a manta ray night dive/snorkel — it's a bucket-list experience
Tour a Kona coffee farm along the scenic Coffee Belt on Hualalai's slopes
Kailua Pier area is great for sunset strolls and casual dining
Two Step (Honaunau Bay) is one of the best snorkeling spots on the island
Magic Sands Beach (La'aloa) is a fun body-surfing beach that literally disappears in winter

hilo.name: Hilo
highlights: Rainbow Falls right in town
Authentic Hawaiian town with no resort crowds
Hilo Farmers Market — one of the best in Hawaii
Gateway to Volcanoes National Park
Lush, tropical rainforest setting
hilo.transport: Fly into Hilo International Airport (ITO) on the Big Island's east coast, or drive 2.5 hours from Kona. Downtown is walkable. A rental car is needed for surrounding attractions.
tips: Hilo is the rainy side of the island — pack a rain jacket but don't let it deter you
The Hilo Farmers Market is best on Wednesday and Saturday mornings
Rainbow Falls is a quick, easy stop just minutes from downtown
Accommodation in Hilo is significantly cheaper than the Kona side
Visit the Pacific Tsunami Museum to understand Hilo's dramatic history

volcanoes-national-park.name: Hawaii Volcanoes National Park
highlights: Kilauea — one of the world's most active volcanoes
Thurston Lava Tube walk-through
Chain of Craters Road to the coast
Devastation Trail through volcanic landscapes
Halemaumau Crater glow at night
volcanoes-national-park.transport: 45-minute drive from Hilo, 2.5 hours from Kona via Saddle Road. $30 per vehicle entry fee valid for 7 days. Volcano House is the only in-park lodge.
tips: Check the NPS website for current eruption status before visiting
Visit at dusk to see the glow of Halemaumau Crater — it's magical after dark
The Kilauea Iki trail descends into a crater and crosses a solidified lava lake
Bring layers — the park sits at 4,000 feet and temperatures are cooler
Chain of Craters Road ends where lava crossed it — the drive down is spectacular

mauna-kea.name: Mauna Kea
highlights: Summit observatories at nearly 14,000 feet
Best stargazing in the Northern Hemisphere
Free public stargazing program at the Visitor Center
Sacred mountain in Hawaiian culture
Sunset above the cloud layer
mauna-kea.transport: Access via Saddle Road (Highway 200) between Hilo and Kona. The summit road requires a 4WD vehicle. Guided summit tours are available from both Hilo and Kona.
tips: The Visitor Information Station at 9,200 feet offers free stargazing programs nightly
Summit access requires 4WD — most rental car companies prohibit driving to the top
Altitude sickness is real at 13,796 feet — acclimate at the Visitor Station first
Dress for near-freezing temperatures at the summit, even in summer
Respect the cultural significance — Mauna Kea is sacred to Native Hawaiians

na-pali-coast.name: Na Pali Coast
highlights: Towering 4,000-foot sea cliffs draped in green
Kalalau Trail — one of the world's most dramatic hikes
Zodiac and catamaran boat tours along the coast
Helicopter tours for aerial perspectives
Remote beaches accessible only by sea or trail
na-pali-coast.transport: Access from Kauai's north shore at Ke'e Beach (end of Highway 560). Kalalau Trail permits required through gostateparks.hawaii.gov. Boat tours depart from Port Allen or Hanalei.
tips: Kalalau Trail permits sell out months in advance — plan early
Boat tours run primarily May–September when north shore waters are calm
Helicopter tours offer the most comprehensive views of the entire coast
The first 2 miles to Hanakapi'ai Beach are doable as a day hike without a permit
Bring proper hiking shoes — the trail is muddy, steep, and narrow in sections

waimea-canyon.name: Waimea Canyon
highlights: The Grand Canyon of the Pacific — 10 miles long, 3,600 feet deep
Multiple lookout points along the canyon rim
Hiking trails from easy rim walks to challenging canyon descents
Connects to Koke'e State Park and its cloud forest
Stunning red and green color contrasts
waimea-canyon.transport: Drive up Waimea Canyon Drive (Highway 550) from the town of Waimea on Kauai's west side. About 1 hour from Lihue Airport. No public transit — a rental car is required.
tips: Visit in the morning for the clearest views — clouds often roll in by afternoon
Drive all the way to the Kalalau Lookout at the top for Na Pali Coast views
The Cliff Trail and Canyon Trail loop is the best moderate hike in the area
Stop at the Koke'e Museum and Lodge for information and snacks
Bring a jacket — temperatures at the rim are significantly cooler than the coast

poipu.name: Poipu
highlights: Sunny south shore beaches — Kauai's driest coast
Spouting Horn blowhole natural wonder
Hawaiian monk seal sightings on the beach
Excellent snorkeling at Poipu Beach Park
National Tropical Botanical Garden (Allerton and McBryde)
poipu.transport: 25-minute drive south from Lihue Airport (LIH). Poipu Road leads to the main beach area. A rental car is recommended for exploring the south shore.
tips: Poipu Beach is one of the best family beaches in Hawaii — gentle waves and lifeguards
Visit Spouting Horn at high tide for the most dramatic blowhole displays
The Allerton Garden tour is outstanding — reservations required
Hawaiian monk seals rest on the beach regularly — maintain 50 feet of distance
Brennecke's Beach is great for body boarding right next to Poipu Beach

molokai.name: Molokai
highlights: Hawaii's most Hawaiian island — authentic and uncommercial
Kalaupapa National Historical Park and its powerful history
Papohaku Beach — one of Hawaii's longest white sand beaches
Halawa Valley hike to Moaula Falls
No traffic lights, no high-rises, no chain restaurants
molokai.transport: Short flights from Honolulu or Maui to Molokai Airport (MKK). A rental car is essential. Very limited services — bring supplies and book accommodation in advance.
tips: Molokai is intentionally undeveloped — embrace the slow pace and respect the culture
Kalaupapa tours require advance booking and a guide — no independent visits allowed
The mule ride down to Kalaupapa is a legendary experience
Stock up on groceries — options on island are limited and expensive
Visit Kanemitsu Bakery for famous hot bread served from the back door at night
